rcu.c(3) Library Functions Manual rcu.c(3)

NAME

rcu.c

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct field_option_map_t
Field name to registry option name mapping. struct int_field_ctx_t
Context for integer field updates in RCU updater callback. struct bool_field_ctx_t
Context for boolean field updates in RCU updater callback. struct string_field_ctx_t
Context for string field updates in RCU updater callback. struct double_field_ctx_t
Context for double field updates in RCU updater callback.

Macros


#define MAX_DEFERRED_FREES 64
Deferred free list for old options structs.

Functions


__attribute__ ((constructor))
Constructor to initialize fork handlers at startup. asciichat_error_t options_state_init (void)
asciichat_error_t options_state_set (const options_t *opts)
void options_state_destroy (void)
void options_cleanup_schema (void)
const options_t * options_get (void)
asciichat_error_t options_set_int (const char *field_name, int value)
asciichat_error_t options_set_bool (const char *field_name, bool value)
asciichat_error_t options_set_string (const char *field_name, const char *value)
asciichat_error_t options_set_double (const char *field_name, double value)

Macro Definition Documentation

#define MAX_DEFERRED_FREES 64

Deferred free list for old options structs. We can't immediately free old structs because readers might still be using them. Simple strategy: Keep old pointers in a list, free them at shutdown.

Future improvements:

  • Hazard pointers: Track which structs readers are using
  • Epoch-based reclamation: Free after grace period
  • Reference counting: Free when no readers

Current approach: Never free until shutdown (acceptable for rare updates)

Definition at line 278 of file rcu.c.

Function Documentation

__attribute__ ((constructor) )

Constructor to initialize fork handlers at startup. This is called before main() by the linker, ensuring fork handlers are registered early.

Definition at line 355 of file rcu.c.

355                                                                                   {
356   // Register the child handler that will be called after fork() in the child process
357   pthread_atfork(NULL, NULL, options_rcu_atfork_child);
358 }








void
  options_cleanup_schema (void )


Definition at line 478 of file rcu.c.


478                                   {
479   config_schema_destroy();
480 }






References config_schema_destroy().


Referenced by main().






const
  options_t * options_get (void )


Definition at line 482 of file rcu.c.


482                                    {
483   // Lock-free read with acquire semantics
484   // Guarantees we see all writes made before the pointer was published
485   options_t *current = atomic_ptr_load(&g_options);
486 
487   // If options not yet published or after destruction, return safe static default.
488   // This is a critical fallback that:
489   // 1. Allows early startup code (before options_state_init) to work safely
490   // 2. Allows atexit handlers and cleanup code to call GET_OPTION() safely
491   // 3. Never crashes due to NULL pointer access
492   // 4. Provides sensible defaults for all option fields
493   //
494   // The static default options are:
495   // - Allocated once at startup (static storage)
496   // - Never freed (outlives all dynamically allocated options)
497   // - Thread-safe to read (immutable const data)
498   // - Complete with all OPT_*_DEFAULT values matching options_t_new()
499   if (!current) {
500     return (const options_t *)&g_default_options;
501   }
502 
503   return current;
504 }






References atomic_ptr_load().


Referenced by acds_main(), client_crypto_init(),
    client_main(), discovery_session_process(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), main(),
    nat_detect_quality(), options_set_bool(),
    options_set_double(), options_set_int(),
    options_set_string(), server_crypto_handshake(),
    session_settings_from_options(), terminal_screen_render(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






asciichat_error_t
  options_set_bool (const char * field_name, bool value)


Definition at line 857 of file rcu.c.


857                                                                        {
858   if (!field_name) {
859     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "field_name is NULL");
860     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
861   }
862 
863   // Validate field exists
864   if (strcmp(field_name, "no_compress") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "encode_audio") != 0 &&
865       strcmp(field_name, "flip_x") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "flip_y") != 0 &&
866       strcmp(field_name, "test_pattern") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "no_audio_mixer") != 0 &&
867       strcmp(field_name, "show_capabilities") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "force_utf8") != 0 &&
868       strcmp(field_name, "audio_enabled") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "audio_analysis_enabled") != 0 &&
869       strcmp(field_name, "audio_no_playback") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "stretch") != 0 &&
870       strcmp(field_name, "snapshot_mode") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "strip_ansi") != 0 &&
871       strcmp(field_name, "quiet") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "encrypt_enabled") != 0 &&
872       strcmp(field_name, "no_encrypt") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "discovery") != 0 &&
873       strcmp(field_name, "discovery_expose_ip") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "discovery_insecure") != 0 &&
874       strcmp(field_name, "webrtc") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "lan_discovery") != 0 &&
875       strcmp(field_name, "no_mdns_advertise") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "prefer_webrtc") != 0 &&
876       strcmp(field_name, "no_webrtc") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "webrtc_skip_stun") != 0 &&
877       strcmp(field_name, "webrtc_disable_turn") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "enable_upnp") != 0 &&
878       strcmp(field_name, "require_server_identity") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "require_client_identity") != 0 &&
879       strcmp(field_name, "require_server_verify") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "require_client_verify") != 0 &&
880       strcmp(field_name, "palette_custom_set") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "media_loop") != 0 &&
881       strcmp(field_name, "media_from_stdin") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "auto_width") != 0 &&
882       strcmp(field_name, "auto_height") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "splash_screen") != 0 &&
883       strcmp(field_name, "status_screen") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "matrix_rain") != 0) {
884     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unknown boolean field: %s", field_name);
885     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
886   }
887 
888   // Pre-validate against registry metadata (for cross-field validators)
889   {
890     const options_t *cur = options_get();
891     options_t temp = *cur;
892     // Manually set the field in temp to validate
893     if (strcmp(field_name, "no_compress") == 0)
894       temp.no_compress = value;
895     else if (strcmp(field_name, "encode_audio") == 0)
896       temp.encode_audio = value;
897     else if (strcmp(field_name, "flip_x") == 0)
898       temp.flip_x = value;
899     else if (strcmp(field_name, "flip_y") == 0)
900       temp.flip_y = value;

902       temp.test_pattern = value;
903     else if (strcmp(field_name, "no_audio_mixer") == 0)
904       temp.no_audio_mixer = value;

906       temp.show_capabilities = value;

908       temp.force_utf8 = value;

910       temp.audio_enabled = value;
911     else if (strcmp(field_name, "audio_analysis_enabled") == 0)
912       temp.audio_analysis_enabled = value;

924       temp.encrypt_enabled = value;

940       temp.prefer_webrtc = value;

942       temp.no_webrtc = value;
943     else if (strcmp(field_name, "webrtc_skip_stun") == 0)
944       temp.webrtc_skip_stun = value;
945     else if (strcmp(field_name, "webrtc_disable_turn") == 0)
946       temp.webrtc_disable_turn = value;
947     else if (strcmp(field_name, "enable_upnp") == 0)
948       temp.enable_upnp = value;
949     else if (strcmp(field_name, "require_server_identity") == 0)
950       temp.require_server_identity = value;
951     else if (strcmp(field_name, "require_client_identity") == 0)
952       temp.require_client_identity = value;
953     else if (strcmp(field_name, "require_server_verify") == 0)
954       temp.require_server_verify = value;
955     else if (strcmp(field_name, "require_client_verify") == 0)
956       temp.require_client_verify = value;
957     else if (strcmp(field_name, "palette_custom_set") == 0)
958       temp.palette_custom_set = value;
959     else if (strcmp(field_name, "media_loop") == 0)
960       temp.media_loop = value;
961     else if (strcmp(field_name, "media_from_stdin") == 0)
962       temp.media_from_stdin = value;
963     else if (strcmp(field_name, "auto_width") == 0)
964       temp.auto_width = value;
965     else if (strcmp(field_name, "auto_height") == 0)
966       temp.auto_height = value;
967     else if (strcmp(field_name, "splash_screen") == 0)
968       temp.splash_screen = value;
969     else if (strcmp(field_name, "status_screen") == 0)
970       temp.status_screen = value;
971     else if (strcmp(field_name, "matrix_rain") == 0)
972       temp.matrix_rain = value;
973 
974     asciichat_error_t err = rcu_validate_field(field_name, &temp);
975     if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) return err;
976   }
977 
978   bool_field_ctx_t ctx = {.field_name = field_name, .value = value};
979   return options_update(bool_field_updater, &ctx);
980 }

asciichat_error_t
  options_set_double (const char * field_name, double value)


Definition at line 1128 of file rcu.c.


1128                                                                            {
1129   if (!field_name) {
1130     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "field_name is NULL");
1131     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
1132   }
1133 
1134   // Normalize option names to internal field names
1135   const char *internal_name = field_name;
1136   if (strcmp(field_name, "microphone-volume") == 0 || strcmp(field_name, "ivolume") == 0) {
1137     internal_name = "microphone_sensitivity";
1138   } else if (strcmp(field_name, "speakers-volume") == 0 || strcmp(field_name, "volume") == 0) {
1139     internal_name = "speakers_volume";
1140   }
1141 
1142   if (strcmp(internal_name, "snapshot_delay") != 0 && strcmp(internal_name, "microphone_sensitivity") != 0 &&
1143       strcmp(internal_name, "speakers_volume") != 0) {
1144     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unknown double field: %s", field_name);
1145     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
1146   }
1147 
1148   // Pre-validate against registry metadata (if available)
1149   {
1150     const options_t *cur = options_get();
1151     options_t temp = *cur;
1152     // Manually set the field in temp to validate
1153     if (strcmp(internal_name, "snapshot_delay") == 0)
1154       temp.snapshot_delay = value;
1155     else if (strcmp(internal_name, "microphone_sensitivity") == 0)
1156       temp.microphone_sensitivity = value;
1157     else if (strcmp(internal_name, "speakers_volume") == 0)
1158       temp.speakers_volume = value;
1159 
1160     asciichat_error_t err = rcu_validate_field(internal_name, &temp);
1161     if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) return err;
1162   }
1163 
1164   double_field_ctx_t ctx = {.field_name = internal_name, .value = value};
1165   return options_update(double_field_updater, &ctx);
1166 }

748     else if (strcmp(field_name, "color_filter") == 0)

1033   if (!field_name) {
1034     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "field_name is NULL");

1037 
1038   if (!value) {

1040     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;

1042 
1043   // Validate field exists
1044   if (strcmp(field_name, "address") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "address6") != 0 &&
1045       strcmp(field_name, "encrypt_key") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "password") != 0 &&
1046       strcmp(field_name, "encrypt_keyfile") != 0 && strcmp(field_name, "server_key") != 0 &&

1058   // Pre-validate against registry metadata (for cross-field validators)

1060     const options_t *cur = options_get();

1063     if (strcmp(field_name, "address") == 0)

1066       SAFE_STRNCPY(temp.address6, value, sizeof(temp.address6));

1082       SAFE_STRNCPY(temp.discovery_database_path, value, sizeof(temp.discovery_database_path));
1083     else if (strcmp(field_name, "log_file") == 0)
1084       SAFE_STRNCPY(temp.log_file, value, sizeof(temp.log_file));
1085     else if (strcmp(field_name, "media_file") == 0)
1086       SAFE_STRNCPY(temp.media_file, value, sizeof(temp.media_file));
1087     else if (strcmp(field_name, "palette_custom") == 0)

void
  options_state_destroy (void )


Definition at line 447 of file rcu.c.


447                                  {
448   // Check if initialized using lifecycle
449   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle)) {
450     return;
451   }
452 
453   /* Shutdown lifecycle to mark as no longer initialized */
454   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
455 
456   // Get current options pointer
457   options_t *current = atomic_ptr_load(&g_options);
458 
459   // Clear the atomic pointer (prevent further reads)
460   atomic_ptr_store(&g_options, NULL);
461 
462   // Free current struct
463   SAFE_FREE(current);
464 
465   // Free all deferred structs
466   deferred_free_all();
467 
468   // Cleanup schema (frees all dynamically allocated config strings)
469   config_schema_destroy();
470 
471   // Destroy dynamically created mutexes
472   mutex_destroy(&g_options_write_mutex);
473   mutex_destroy(&g_deferred_free_mutex);
474 
475   log_debug("Options state shutdown complete");
476 }






References atomic_ptr_load(), atomic_ptr_store(),
    config_schema_destroy(), lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    lifecycle_shutdown(), and mutex_destroy().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy(),
    client_cleanup(), main(), and mirror_cleanup().






asciichat_error_t
  options_state_init (void )


Definition at line 365 of file rcu.c.


365                                            {
366   // Detect fork: after fork(), child process must reinitialize
367   pid_t current_pid = platform_get_pid();
368   if ((lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle) || g_init_pid != -1) && g_init_pid != current_pid) {
369     log_debug("Detected fork in options_state_init (parent PID %d, child PID %d) - resetting lifecycle", g_init_pid,
370               current_pid);
371     /* Reset lifecycle to allow reinitialization after fork */
372     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
373     g_init_pid = current_pid;
374   }
375 
376   // Check if already initialized in this process using lifecycle
377   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle) && g_init_pid == current_pid) {
378     log_warn("Options state already initialized");
379     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
380   }
381 
382   /* Use lifecycle to serialize initialization */
383   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_options_lifecycle, "options")) {
384     /* Another thread is initializing or already initialized */
385     if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle)) {
386       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
387     }
388     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to acquire options initialization lock");
389   }
390 
391   // Initialize write mutex
392   if (mutex_init(&g_options_write_mutex, "options_write") != 0) {
393     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
394     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize options write mutex");
395   }
396 
397   // Initialize deferred free mutex
398   if (mutex_init(&g_deferred_free_mutex, "deferred_free") != 0) {
399     mutex_destroy(&g_options_write_mutex);
400     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
401     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize deferred free mutex");
402   }
403 
404   // Allocate initial options struct (will be populated later by options_state_populate_from_globals)
405   options_t *initial_opts = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(options_t), options_t *);
406   if (!initial_opts) {
407     mutex_destroy(&g_options_write_mutex);
408     mutex_destroy(&g_deferred_free_mutex);
409     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_options_lifecycle);
410     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate initial options struct");
411   }
412 
413   // Initialize all defaults using options_t_new()
414   *initial_opts = options_t_new();
415 
416   // Publish initial struct (release semantics - make all fields visible to readers)
417   atomic_ptr_store(&g_options, initial_opts);
418 
419   g_init_pid = current_pid; // Record PID to detect fork later
420   log_dev("Options state initialized with RCU pattern (PID %d)", current_pid);
421 
422   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
423 }






References atomic_ptr_store(), lifecycle_init(),
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), lifecycle_shutdown(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), options_t_new(), and
    platform_get_pid().


Referenced by __attribute__(), and
  options_init().






asciichat_error_t
  options_state_set (const options_t * opts)


Definition at line 425 of file rcu.c.


425                                                            {
426   if (!opts) {
427     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "opts is NULL");
428   }
429 
430   // Check initialization state using lifecycle
431   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_options_lifecycle)) {
432     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Options state not initialized (call options_state_init first)");
433   }
434 
435   // Get current struct (should be the initial zero-initialized one during startup)
436   options_t *current = atomic_ptr_load(&g_options);
437 
438   // Copy provided options into current struct
439   // Note: No need for RCU update here since this is called during initialization
440   // before any reader threads exist
441   memcpy(current, opts, sizeof(options_t));
442 
443   log_debug("Options state set from parsed struct");
444   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
445 }






References atomic_ptr_load(), and
    lifecycle_is_initialized().


Referenced by config_load_and_apply(), and
    options_init().

    Version 0.9.0
